metric: distinct format/entry-type state sequences (class 2), via targets/archive_describe
reference_annotation: IJON_STATE(hash(filter_code, format, entry_filetype)) per decoded header

ab_deterministic.note: plain control vs reference IJON annotation, 120s/arm, 4 seeds, single core
ab_deterministic.honest_scope: magnitude is budget-dependent and amplified by IJON corpus retention (every new state sequence is saved as 'new coverage'); the durable point is the mechanism: plain AFL structurally discards state-equivalent inputs, IJON retains and keeps mutating them

autonomous_loop.note: scripts/run_target.py, blind (fairness-stripped 54-line harness), plateau 75s, eval 90s/iter, deepseek-v4-pro
autonomous_loop.diagnosis: known_state_changes (class 2) — 'edge coverage sees the same loop code for each header; no gradient'

iter2.annotation: { static int seq=0; seq=ijon_hashint(seq, archive_entry_filetype(entry)); IJON_STATE(seq); }
iter2.why: richer sequence/history idiom, but did not beat iter1 in the 90s window — coverage-blind reward correctly did not credit it

takeaway: On a fresh real target, the agent blind-diagnosed the class-2 roadblock and re-derived the entry-type state annotation; the deterministic A/B shows the headroom (filter+format+type sequence -> 233x). Same gradient as libpng (sequences on shared code) and libtpms (command orderings).
